description_long: This rifle features a distinctive boxy receiver with a rounded trigger guard and a prominent, elongated barrel extending from the main body. The grip is angled and ergonomic, positioned below the receiver, and the trigger is housed within a large, open trigger guard. The upper section of the receiver includes mechanical details such as a rear sight and bolt assembly, while the barrel is long and slender, giving the weapon a classic, historical appearance. The magazine is integrated into the lower receiver, flush with the body, and the overall silhouette is compact yet elongated due to the barrel. The finish appears to be a uniform dark color, likely blued or parkerized metal, with minimal external embellishments. This rifle is reminiscent of early 20th-century military sidearms, such as the Mauser C96.
description: A classic, boxy rifle with a long barrel, integrated magazine, and ergonomic grip, finished in a dark, uniform color. The design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military firearms.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, boxy receiver, and ergonomic grip.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow barrel profile and the alignment of the sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the main features of the receiver, barrel, and grip.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the grip, rear sight, and the back of the receiver.
